David waited with a secret hope that the Lord would help him out of his difficulty. He seemed to be influenced too much by the fear of people, in agreeing to attend Achish. It is hard to come close to sin and not fall into it. God inclined the princes of the Philistines to oppose David’s being used in the battle. Their dislike befriended him, when no friend could do such a kindness for him.

David scarcely ever had a greater deliverance than when he was dismissed from that deceptive service. God's people should always behave themselves so that, if possible, they get a good word from everyone they have dealings with; and it is due to those who have acted well to speak well of them.
